```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  pa <- sample(seq(0.55,0.90,0.05),1)
  pb <- sample(seq(0.55,0.90,0.05),1)
  lower <- pa+pb-1
  vals <- round(c(lower,pa*pb,min(pa,pb),1-pa-pb,1-lower),3)
  if(length(unique(vals))==5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Sabe-se apenas que $P(A)=`r pa`$ e $P(B)=`r pb`$. Qual é o limite inferior de Bonferroni para $P(A\cap B)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Como $P(A\cup B)\leq1$,
$$P(A\cap B)\geq P(A)+P(B)-1
=`r sprintf("%.3f",lower)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S3Q10_Bonferroni_intersecao
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
